content: Getting data, whatever that means for your project, is usually the first step. So let's access some basic data
Agency copilot is internal only, and gives you access to MCP servers and skills
Confirm Folder Trust - human in the loop for safety! Click #2 and trust work folders
WorkIQ - 'intelligence layer' between you/AI and M365 data
Permissions again, ugh. Is it going to ask me to do everything? Yes.
Reduce some cognitive load. '/experimental on' and then '/yolo auto'
Sometimes, even if an agent is set up to have MCP servers and connections like WorkIQ, you must specifically name them in your prompt
No connector and no download button? Tell the agent the exact URL and what you'd like it to read. Success depends on the accessibility/readability of the website.
Let's get some larger and more complex data sets and show how we can use AI to process this data...intelligently

M365 chat hallucinates totals, or needs effort from Microsoft to ingest grounded, properly referenced answers. A CLI agent downloads the real file and computes real answers. Writing python/powershell scripts are more flexible and reusable.
Chat will guess which country "feels" like it's rising. The agent subtracts two real columns for every economy and ranks them - no vibes, just the delta.
Give the agent only the blog post. It has to navigate to the public data itself, then verify — and honestly flag any claim the data can't confirm.
If at first you don't succeed try again. Then your courage should appear, For if you will persevere, You will conquer, never fear; Try, try again. (Sharepoint MCP times out frequently)
Vague prompts will usually give you vague/default results.
Stating your high level goals ahead of your more specific instructions give the AI context to tailor results to your need
You can build projects
Turn on Plan Mode (Shift+Tab OR /plan), read the plan it proposes, then approve it to run.
Plan mode (or Spec-Kit) are an easy way for you to transition from small projects to big projects. Work on the Plan/Spec with the agent, then go back to your work til its done.
When a Plan/Spec is created, take your time to review - these are human readable files
Plan first, review, then let it build. The prize is a reusable tool, not a one-off answer.
Plan it, review the steps, then run. Python can serve a folder locally on localhost - no cloud, no deploy, nothing leaves your laptop.
